Fix three concrete bugs in the Vite React TS app at `/Users/golamrabbani/expatal/PropContext/ui/`. Server is already running at http://localhost:8000 — do NOT restart it. Just rebuild with `npm run build` after edits and reload.

## Bug 1 — Frontmatter rendering broken (highest priority)

`ui/src/lib/markdown.ts` uses `gray-matter`, which depends on Node `Buffer` and silently throws in the browser. Its `try/catch` falls back to `{ data: {}, content: raw }` — so the FrontmatterCard renders nothing useful, and the body still contains the literal `---\nname: ...\ndescription: ...\n---` frontmatter block (rendered as a serif paragraph in the screenshot the user just shared).

Fix by replacing `gray-matter` with a tiny browser-safe parser. Don't add any new dep. Inline parser, ~25 lines:

```ts
const FM_RE = /^---\r?\n([\s\S]*?)\r?\n---\r?\n?/;

export function parseMarkdown(raw: string): ParsedDoc {
  const m = raw.match(FM_RE);
  if (!m) return { data: {}, content: raw };
  const yaml = m[1];
  const content = raw.slice(m[0].length);
  const data: Record<string, string> = {};
  let key = "";
  for (const line of yaml.split(/\r?\n/)) {
    const kv = /^([A-Za-z_][\w-]*)\s*:\s*(.*)$/.exec(line);
    if (kv) {
      key = kv[1];
      data[key] = kv[2].trim();
    } else if (key && /^\s+/.test(line)) {
      data[key] = (data[key] + " " + line.trim()).trim();
    }
  }
  return { data, content };
}
```

Then **uninstall** gray-matter from `package.json` (`npm uninstall gray-matter`). Update `Frontmatter` type to `Record<string, string>` since the inline parser returns strings only — that's fine for our YAML which is flat key/string.

The wiki frontmatter looks like this (real example):

```
---
name: property-lie-001
description: Property LIE-001 (WEG Immanuelkirchstraße 26, ...). Read first for any agent task touching this Liegenschaft. Lists buildings, ...
---
```

Verify after rebuild: load `http://localhost:8000/` and the index.md `description` field should be in the FrontmatterCard, NOT in the body prose.

## Bug 2 — Theme toggle does nothing

The theme toggle button (sun/moon icon top-right) isn't wired to actual theme switching. Two acceptable approaches:

- **Approach A (preferred):** toggle a `dark` class on `<html>` and use Tailwind v4's `@variant dark` for dark-mode styles. Persist to `localStorage` under key `theme` (values: `dark` | `light`). Default to `dark` on first load if no preference saved.
- **Approach B:** if the design currently uses CSS custom properties for tokens, swap a `data-theme="light"` attribute on `<html>` and define an alternate token set under `[data-theme="light"]`.

Whichever approach, the toggle button must:
- read current state from a `useState` initialized from `localStorage.getItem("theme")` (default `"dark"`)
- on click, flip state, write to localStorage, apply the class/attribute to `document.documentElement`
- swap the lucide icon (`Sun` / `Moon`) accordingly

Also support OS preference on first load: if no saved theme, use `window.matchMedia("(prefers-color-scheme: light)").matches` to decide initial theme.

If the existing design only has dark-mode tokens defined, you need to add a light-mode palette too. Use:
- bg: `#f9f9f7` (warm off-white, complements the existing zinc-black)
- fg: `#0b0b0a`
- muted: `#5a5a55`
- border: rgba(0,0,0,0.08)
- accent: keep `#d4ff3c` (chartreuse works in both themes — slightly desaturate to `#a8d122` for light if it's too harsh)

## Bug 3 — Resizable panels leak rotated headers when collapsed

The screenshot shows the Tree panel collapsed to ~30px wide with the word "TREE" displayed as rotated/squished vertical text on the left edge. Same on the right Ask panel. This happens because:
- `react-resizable-panels` `<Panel>` has `collapsible={true}` + `collapsedSize={0}` but the panel content (a sticky header with a "TREE" / "ASK THE WIKI" label) overflows when the panel becomes very narrow.
- Or, more likely, the panel is at its `minSize` (not actually collapsed), and the inner content overflows.

Fixes:
- **Increase minSizes** so panels can't visually break: tree `minSize={14}` (with `collapsedSize={0}`), ask `minSize={18}` (with `collapsedSize={0}`).
- **Add `overflow: hidden`** on each panel's outer wrapper so children clip cleanly.
- **Hide the content entirely when collapsed.** Use the imperative `panelRef` `onCollapse` / `onExpand` callbacks (or the `onResize` size to detect `< minSize`) and conditionally render the inner header. When collapsed, render nothing — let the panel be 0px so the resize handle alone is visible.
- **Only render the panel label when expanded.** Don't show vertical-rotated text or sideways labels when collapsed; just show the resize handle.
- Verify on a wide window (1920px+) the default proportions don't put the tree below its readable threshold. Bump default tree size from 18 → 20 if it looks cramped.

Also fix the responsive issue: at narrow viewport widths (< 900px), the three-panel layout becomes unusable. Add a CSS media query: below 768px, hide both side panels by default and show only the viewer (the user can still toggle them via the topbar buttons). Use `@media (max-width: 768px) { .ask-panel, .tree-panel { display: none } }` or equivalent — adjust to your component structure.

2. Edit `src/lib/markdown.ts` (replace gray-matter usage). `npm uninstall gray-matter`.
3. Wire theme toggle in `src/components/TopBar.tsx` + add the corresponding CSS in `src/styles.css` or wherever theme tokens live. Initialize on app mount (in `App.tsx` `useEffect`).
